content: A carrier wave of peak voltage 16 V is used to transmit a signal. If the modulation index is $75 \%$, the peak voltage of the modulating signal is
explanation:
Modulation Index

$$(\mathrm{MI})=\frac{\text { Peak voltage of modulating signal }\left(V_m\right)}{\text { Peak voltage of carrier signal }\left(V_c\right)}$$

Given, $\mathrm{MI}=75 \%$ and $V_c=16 \mathrm{~V}$

$$\therefore \quad V_m=\mathrm{MI} \times V_c=\frac{75}{100} \times 16=12 \mathrm{~V}$$

content: In communication system, a repeater is used to extend the range to transmission. It is the combination of
explanation: A repeater is a combination of at least one transmitter, one receiver and one carrier operated relay (COR) configured in a manner where a signal is received and transmitter rebroadcasts the received signal on another frequency.

content: In amplitude modulation, the amplitude of carrier wave is $A_c$ and that of the modulating signal is $A_m$. In practice, the ratio of $A_m$ to $A_c$ is kept less than or equal to one, to avoid
explanation: Modulation index, $\mu=\frac{A_m}{A_c} \leq 1$ is carried by the carrier wave, i.e. there is no signal distortion.

options.content: both frequency and amplitude vary with time
both frequency and amplitude are constant

explanation:
In frequency modulated wave, only frequency varies with time, while the amplitude remains constant. This wave can be shown as

It is used in FM broadcasting, telemetry and RADAR etc.

content: A layer of atmosphere that reflects medium frequency radio waves which is ineffective during night, is
explanation:
The atmospheric $E$ region or $E$ layer is above the $D$-region and below the F-region. It exists at altitude between 100 to 125 km above the earth surface $E$-layer assists in long distance radio communication.

In this layer, gases like oxygen and nitrogen are ionized due to photo-ionization by energy released from the sun during day time which facilitates in radio communication so, this layer does not work during in the night time.
